title: Royal Australian Navy Expands Indo-Pacific Presence Through Exercise Kakadu 2026 and Balikatan Partnership
summary: The Royal Australian Navy continued strengthening regional partnerships through Exercise Kakadu 2026 off Australia's eastern coast and HMAS Toowoomba's participation in Balikatan 2026, demonstrating expanded interoperability with 18 partner nations.
contents:
## Australian Naval Engagement Expands Across Indo-Pacific

### Exercise Kakadu 2026
The Royal Australian Navy hosted Exercise Kakadu 2026, its premier multinational maritime engagement activity, bringing together more than 20 vessels and supporting aircraft from Australia and 18 partner nations. The exercise area expanded to include waters from Darwin to Cairns and down the eastern coast to Jervis Bay, New South Wales. At its peak, more than 6,000 personnel participated in an International Fleet Review on Sydney Harbour.

The USS Fitzgerald (DDG 62) represented the U.S. Navy, while ships from India, Malaysia, the Philippines, Thailand, and Vietnam formed a combined task group led by HMAS Choules. The task group conducted officer-of-the-watch manoeuvres, formation foxtrots, and high-end warfare scenarios during transit down Australia's east coast.

### Balikatan 2026 Maritime Component
HMAS Toowoomba participated in the maritime component of Exercise Balikatan 2026, marking the first time a major Royal Australian Navy fleet unit joined these exercises. Ten warships from five partner nations — Australia, the Philippines, the United States, Japan, and Canada — sailed as one fleet during the largest maritime cooperative activity the Philippines attempted.

Commander Alicia Harrison, Commanding Officer of Toowoomba, noted that having officers from partner nations at the con during complex exercises demonstrated the trust shared among regional partners. The deployment formed part of Australia's Regional Presence Deployment 26-2, which departed Fleet Base West in early February.

### Strategic Significance
These exercises demonstrate Australia's commitment to upholding international law, including freedom of navigation as reflected in the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea. Australia reaffirmed the 2016 South China Sea Arbitral Tribunal Award as final and legally binding on the parties.

title: NSRI Conducts Complex Maritime Medical Evacuation From Chemical Tanker Off Cape Town
summary: South Africa's National Sea Rescue Institute executed a high-angle medical evacuation of an injured seafarer from a Hong Kong-flagged chemical tanker in 3-metre swells, 12 nautical miles west of Green Point, Cape Town.
contents:
## Maritime Medical Emergency Off Table Bay

### Distress Response
At 01:19 on Tuesday May 5, 2026, NSRI Table Bay duty crew activated alongside Western Cape Government Health EMS after receiving a distress call from a Hong Kong-flagged chemical tanker offshore. A 36-year-old Myanmar crewman suffered a serious medical emergency on board.

An EMS duty doctor linked via control assessed the situation remotely and determined that paramedics needed to reach the patient directly, requiring hospital care as soon as possible.

### Rescue Operation
NSRI launched its rescue craft DHL Deliverer from the Table Bay rescue base. Onboard Maritime Extrication specialists and EMS rescue paramedics headed into 3-metre swells toward a rendezvous point 12 nautical miles west of Green Point. Radio communications maintained through Telkom Maritime Radio Services kept all parties connected throughout the operation.

When the rescue craft reached the tanker, the challenging sea state complicated every transfer. An NSRI MEX crewman and an EMS paramedic transferred onto the vessel carrying critical medical and extrication equipment. Treatment commenced immediately to stabilize the patient before any attempt to move him.

### Technical High-Angle Extrication
Using specialized gear brought across from the rescue craft, the team secured the crewman into a Baumann bag and onto a scoop stretcher. A technical high-angle extrication followed, coordinated with the ship's master and crew. The patient transferred carefully from the tanker onto the waiting NSRI rescue craft below.

The crewman reached the NSRI base at Table Bay by 06:15, where an ambulance transported him to hospital in a serious but stable condition. The Maritime Rescue Coordination Centre coordinated with NSRI controllers, EMS Metro Control, Port of Table Bay authorities, Port Health officials, and the vessel's crew throughout the operation.

title: NATO Open Spirit 2026 Clears Legacy Mines in Baltic Sea Off Latvia
summary: NATO and partner navies concluded Exercise Open Spirit 2026 off Latvia, bringing together ships from eight nations, nine diving teams, and five autonomous underwater vehicle teams to detect and neutralize World War-era ordnance in the Baltic Sea.
contents:
## NATO Mine Countermeasures in the Baltic

### Exercise Overview
NATO ships participated in Exercise Open Spirit 26, a major international mine countermeasures drill off Latvia's coast from 1 to 13 May 2026. The exercise brought together ships from eight nations, supported by nine diving teams and five autonomous underwater vehicle teams. Nearly 400 military personnel took part, including Standing NATO Mine Countermeasures Group 1 (SNMCMG1) led by Polish Navy flagship ORP Kontradmiral Xawery Czernicki.

### Operational Impact
Unlike many exercises, Open Spirit delivered a real-world operational effect. Every mine or explosive remnant located and neutralized immediately improved safety for commercial shipping, fishing, and local communities. Using advanced underwater systems, participating forces conducted detailed identification of suspected objects and safely disposed of confirmed mines and unexploded ordnance from both World Wars.

### Coordination With Aurora 26
The exercise coordinated closely with Sweden's national defense exercise Aurora 26, linking mine warfare with wider regional defense efforts. This strengthened international cooperation and enhanced the interoperability, tactics, and procedures of mine countermeasure vessels, diving teams, and command elements.

Commander SNMCMG1, Polish Navy Commander Kacper Sterne stated that Open Spirit provided an ideal opportunity to enhance capabilities in detecting and countermining sea mines from past conflicts while maintaining a high level of operational readiness. The exercise rotates annually between the Baltic States, reinforcing regional ownership and cooperation across the Baltic region.

title: Japan Deploys 1,400 Combat Troops to Balikatan 2026, Fires Live Anti-Ship Missiles in Philippines
summary: Japan's Self-Defense Forces transitioned from observer to active participant in Balikatan 2026, deploying 1,400 combat troops and test-firing Type 88 surface-to-ship missiles in the Philippines — the first offensive missile launch by Japanese forces overseas since World War II.
contents:
## Japan's Historic Military Deployment

### First Combat Troop Deployment Abroad
Japan took part in Balikatan 2026 on an unprecedented scale, transitioning from observer status to deploying 1,400 combat troops. Analysts described the move as reflecting the emergence of a more integrated regional approach to Indo-Pacific security and collective deterrence. Japan's expanded participation followed the ratification of a Reciprocal Access Agreement on September 11, 2025.

### Live Anti-Ship Missile Exercise
On May 6, Japanese troops fired live Type 88 anti-ship missiles in the Philippines, targeting a decommissioned Philippine ship approximately 75 kilometers away in the South China Sea. The missile exercise involved 70 Japanese personnel and marked the first time since World War II that Japanese forces launched offensive missiles overseas.

### China's Response
Beijing sharply criticized Japan's expanded role in the exercises. Chinese Foreign Ministry spokespersons Lin Jian and Guo Jiakun accused Japan of accelerating remilitarization and undermining its pacifist constitution. Beijing declared it unacceptable for a "former aggressor country" to launch offensive missiles abroad under the pretext of security cooperation.

### Strategic Significance
Retired Indian Air Marshal Anil Khosla described Japan's role as ending the "observer era" and reflecting that Japan now normalizes greater overseas engagement. The deployment improved interoperability and exercised capabilities relevant to defending Japan's southwestern islands and supporting allies across the Indo-Pacific.

title: PLA Southern Theater Command Conducts Combat Readiness Patrols Around Scarborough Shoal
summary: China's PLA Southern Theater Command conducted combat readiness patrols in the territorial sea and airspace around Huangyan Dao (Scarborough Shoal) in response to the multinational Balikatan 2026 exercises involving seven nations near the Philippines.
contents:
## PLA Responds to Regional Military Exercises

### Combat Readiness Patrols
On April 30, 2026, the navy and air force troops assigned to the Chinese PLA Southern Theater Command conducted combat readiness patrols in the territorial sea and airspace of China's Huangyan Dao and its surrounding areas. The STC announced that forces had strengthened combat readiness patrols since April in response to what it characterized as rights-violation and provocative acts.

### Deployment Scale
The Armed Forces of the Philippines reported monitoring 35 Chinese vessels across West Philippine Sea features from May 4 to 11, including PLAN ships and China Coast Guard vessels near Ayungin Shoal, Bajo de Masinloc, Escoda Shoal, and the Pag-asa Islands. The AFP stated it did not observe aggressive or coercive actions during the Balikatan 2026 exercises.

### Naval Task Group Operations
The PLA also deployed a surface task group consisting of one Type 055 guided-missile destroyer, one Type 052D guided-missile destroyer, one Type 054A guided-missile frigate, and one Type 903A auxiliary oil replenishment vessel east of the Luzon Strait. The aircraft carrier Liaoning transited the Taiwan Strait heading south on April 20.

### Official Statement
Senior Colonel Zhang Xiaogang, spokesperson for the Chinese Ministry of National Defense, stated at an April 30 press conference that the Asia-Pacific region needs peace and stability rather than outside powers stirring up division. The patrols aimed to safeguard China's territorial sovereignty and uphold peace and stability in the South China Sea, according to the official statement.

title: North Korea Plans 155mm Self-Propelled Howitzer Deployment Along Southern Border by Year-End
summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong-un visited munitions factories and revealed plans to deploy a new 155mm self-propelled gun-howitzer with a range exceeding 60 kilometers along the southern border, placing Seoul within striking distance.
contents:
## North Korean Artillery Buildup and Military Modernization

### New Howitzer Deployment
North Korean leader Kim Jong-un visited a munitions factory on May 6 to review production of a new-type 155mm self-propelled gun-howitzer destined for three battalions stationed at a long-range artillery unit along the southern border. According to the Korean Central News Agency, the system has a striking range exceeding 60 kilometers, enough to place Seoul within firing range of North Korean front-line positions.

### Destroyer Commissioning
Kim also observed a maneuvering assessment test aboard the 5,000-ton warship Choe Hyon on May 7 ahead of its commissioning, ordering delivery to the navy by mid-June as scheduled. Pyongyang previously conducted strategic cruise missile tests from the destroyer, which analysts believe may carry nuclear warheads. Kim's daughter Ju-ae accompanied him on the inspection.

### Munitions Production Expansion
During a follow-up visit on May 11, Kim called for strengthening mortar and howitzer forces and establishing a specialized artillery production complex and a small-arms production factory. Kim praised workers for achieving record growth in shell and bullet production and ordered a production system for high-precision multipurpose bullets of various calibers.

### Constitutional Amendments
South Korea's Ministry of Unification revealed that North Korea amended its constitution to remove all references to national reunification, define its territorial borders as bordering China and Russia to the north and South Korea to the south, and grant the chairman of the State Affairs Commission explicit command authority over nuclear forces for the first time.

title: Russia Launches Largest Two-Day Aerial Attack on Ukraine Since Invasion, Killing 24 in Kyiv
summary: Russia launched over 1,560 drones and 56 missiles against Ukrainian cities in its heaviest wartime drone attack, destroying a nine-storey residential building in Kyiv and killing at least 24 people including three children.
contents:
## Massive Russian Aerial Assault on Ukraine

### Scale of Attack
Russia carried out its largest aerial attack over a two-day period since the start of the war, launching 1,567 drones and 56 missiles at Ukrainian cities. President Volodymyr Zelensky described the assault as targeting more than 180 sites across Ukraine, including more than 50 residential buildings.

### Kyiv Devastation
The overnight barrage on May 14 struck Kyiv hardest. A Kh-101 cruise missile leveled an entire section of a nine-storey apartment building in the Darnytskyi district, destroying 18 flats. Emergency services pulled 24 bodies from the rubble, including three girls aged 12 and 15. Among the victims, 12-year-old Lyubava Yakovleva lost her father during the war — her elder sister also perished in the strike.

### Rescue Operations
More than 1,500 rescuers and police officers deployed across Ukraine, nearly 600 of them in Kyiv. Teams moved more than 20 cubic metres of debris and rescued 30 people alive. Mayor Vitali Klitschko declared a day of mourning in the capital.

### Air Defense Performance
Ukraine's air force shot down 652 of the drones and 41 missiles, achieving an overall interception rate of 93 percent for drones but only 73 percent for missiles. Zelensky identified the key challenge as defending against Russian ballistic missiles and urged allies to provide more anti-ballistic systems.

### International Condemnation
EU Commission President Ursula von der Leyen accused Moscow of indiscriminately targeting civilians and announced finalization of a 6 billion euro drone support package. French President Emmanuel Macron stated that Russia demonstrates weakness through bombing civilians rather than strength.

title: U.S. Missile Defense Agency Plans Project Maverick Counter-Hypersonic Test in Fiscal 2027
summary: The U.S. Missile Defense Agency revealed Project Maverick, a planned flight test to demonstrate tracking and defeating hypersonic missiles by fiscal 2027, aiming to provide an interim counter-hypersonic capability before the Glide Phase Interceptor arrives in 2031.
contents:
## Project Maverick Counter-Hypersonic Initiative

### Test Architecture
The Missile Defense Agency plans to conduct a flight test called Project Maverick in fiscal 2027 to demonstrate the ability to track and defeat hypersonic missiles. According to budget documents, a hypersonic glide vehicle will fly up the East Coast while MDA tracks it with multi-phenomenology elevated sensor data from air and space-based platforms. A tactical battle management system will process and fuse the data, enabling engage-on-remote capability.

### Interim Capability
MDA Director Lt. Gen. Heath Collins stated in testimony to the House Armed Services Committee that Project Maverick provides a developmental test opportunity to demonstrate capabilities across the kill chain. Successfully demonstrated capabilities would supplement current and future defense architectures, filling the gap until the more advanced Glide Phase Interceptor arrives in 2031.

### Low-Cost Defeat Strategy
Project Maverick operates under MDA's new Low-Cost Defeat initiative, which includes the Low-Cost Interceptor program aimed at proving an affordable interceptor buildable in high volumes. An LCI prototype demonstration targets 2028. Together, MDA seeks $460 million in fiscal 2027 for these efforts.

### Golden Dome Connection
The initiative connects to the Pentagon's broader Golden Dome missile defense architecture, projected at $185 billion, which envisions a layered network of sensors, missile interceptors, and command-and-control systems across domains. Golden Dome Director Gen. Michael Guetlein confirmed plans for a missile interceptor demonstration by next summer. The fiscal 2027 budget request includes $17.9 billion for Golden Dome programs.

title: Ukrainian Hornet Drones Interdict Russian Supply Lines Near Occupied Mariupol
summary: Ukrainian drones now operate freely over the T-0509 Mariupol-Donetsk highway, targeting Russian tanker trucks and military transport vehicles in what amounts to battlefield air interdiction reaching 200 kilometers into Russian-held rear areas.
contents:
## Ukrainian Drone Operations Reach Russian Rear

### Battlefield Air Interdiction
The Ukrainian 1st Azov National Guard Corps published footage on May 8 showing Ukrainian drones reconnoitering and interdicting Russian ground lines of communication through Mariupol. Ukrainian Hornet drones now operate freely over the T-0509 Mariupol-Donetsk City highway, targeting Russian tanker trucks and other military transport vehicles.

### Expanding Operational Depth
Russian military bloggers expressed serious concern that the situation along supply lines near Mariupol now resembles conditions along the M-30 Horlivka-Yasynuvata-Donetsk highway, where Ukrainian forces reportedly paralyzed Russian logistics and civilian traffic using drones. The M-30 highway, despite sitting 35 kilometers from the frontline, proved less safe in 2026 than during 2024-2025 when frontlines sat closer.

### Starlink-Enabled Range
Ukrainian Starlink-enabled drones with ranges up to 200 kilometers now reach the M-14 Mariupol-Berdyansk-Melitopol-Henichesk highway, a key Russian logistics corridor along the Sea of Azov coast to occupied Crimea. Russian milbloggers warned that electronic warfare can only jam Ukrainian Hornet drones if they do not use Starlink or lack time to auto-lock onto targets.

### Logistics Disruption
Partisans of the Atesh movement reported that Russian command has begun relocating warehouses storing FPV drones and ammunition further from the frontline. A Russian soldier who contacted Atesh stated that storing supplies just a few kilometers from positions now constitutes suicide. Supply chains have grown significantly longer and routes more vulnerable as a result.

### Future Trajectory
Russian analysts expressed concern that Ukrainian forces may fully automate Hornet drones within the next six to twelve months, potentially achieving complete battlefield air interdiction over Russian rear areas by early 2027.

title: Taiwan Legislature Slashes Defense Budget Undermining Porcupine Strategy Ahead of Trump-Xi Summit
summary: Taiwan's opposition-dominated legislature approved only $25 billion of a proposed $40 billion defense budget on May 8, cutting domestic drone procurement critical to the asymmetric porcupine defense strategy against China's growing grey-zone maritime pressure.
contents:
## Taiwan Defense Budget Cut Weakens Maritime Security Posture

### Legislative Decision

Taiwan's legislature approved approximately $25 billion of a proposed $40 billion special supplementary defense budget on May 8, 2026, with China-friendly opposition parties rejecting the remainder. The Kuomintang (KMT) and Taiwan People's Party opposed domestic spending provisions, including funding for 200,000 drones, citing insufficient transparency and corruption risk.

### Impact on Porcupine Strategy

The approved funding covers purchases of US weapons worth approximately $24 billion across two packages, including artillery, Javelin anti-tank missiles, and additional systems. However, the cuts eliminated domestic procurement essential to the "porcupine strategy" — using small, cheap, numerous mobile weapons to defend against China's much larger military. Wars in Ukraine and Iran have demonstrated drones serve as essential components of modern asymmetric defense.

### Grey-Zone Maritime Pressure

The budget decision comes amid escalating Chinese grey-zone maritime pressure. Taiwan's Coast Guard Administration recorded 247 Chinese coast guard incursions into Taiwan-controlled waters in the first four months of 2026 — more than double the total for all of 2024. Chinese vessels patrol beyond Taiwan's twelve-nautical-mile territorial sea, establishing presence and enforcing invisible lines that gradually erode sovereignty.

### Strategic Timing

The cuts coincided with President Trump's planned meeting with President Xi on May 14-15, weakening Taipei's credibility in demonstrating serious commitment to self-defense. A State Department spokesperson characterized further funding delays as "a concession to the Chinese Communist Party."

### Environmental Security Nexus

The maritime grey-zone operations extend to environmental monitoring capabilities, as Chinese coast guard cutters increasingly operate in waters where Taiwan conducts marine resource surveys and environmental monitoring programs.

title: Japan-China Military Rivalry Intensifies After Taiwan Strait Transit and Missile Deployments
summary: Japan-China tensions have escalated sharply following the JS Ikazuchi destroyer transit through the Taiwan Strait and deployment of upgraded Type-12 missiles with 1,000-km range. Beijing responded with combat readiness patrols and diplomatic condemnation.
contents:
## Japan-China Defense Rivalry Escalation

### Strategic Context

The rivalry between Japan and China entered a more dangerous phase in May 2026, driven by cumulative military and diplomatic actions on both sides. The Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force destroyer JS Ikazuchi transited the Taiwan Strait on April 17, marking the second such passage in ten months but the first under Prime Minister Takaichi Sanae's administration.

### Trigger Events

Beijing reacted with unusual severity because the transit date coincided with the anniversary of the Treaty of Shimonoseki (1895), which ceded Taiwan to Japan. China's Foreign Ministry, Defense Ministry, and Eastern Theatre Command all issued condemnation statements. The PLA Eastern Theater Command deployed naval and air forces to track and monitor the Ikazuchi throughout its approximately fourteen-hour passage.

### Military Developments

Japan deployed its first upgraded Type-12 land-to-ship missiles in March 2026, providing standoff capability reaching approximately 1,000 kilometers — sufficient to strike mainland China. Tokyo plans additional missile deployments on Yonaguni, Japan's westernmost island approximately 110 km from Taiwan. China launched combat-readiness patrols in the East China Sea and dispatched warships near Okinawa in response.

### Diplomatic Deterioration

Japan's 2026 Diplomatic Bluebook downgraded China from "one of the most important bilateral relations" to merely an "important neighboring country." Prime Minister Takaichi's November 2025 statement that a Chinese attack on Taiwan could constitute a "survival-threatening situation" for Japan continues to fuel Beijing's anger. China has raised alarms about Japan's nuclear potential at the UN NPT Review Conference.

### Regional Impact

Japan simultaneously deepened defense cooperation with the Philippines through the Reciprocal Access Agreement and Acquisition and Cross-Servicing Agreement, embedding itself more firmly in the regional architecture aimed at constraining China across the First Island Chain.

link_preview.title: China and Japan Are Entering a More Dangerous Phase of Rivalry
link_preview.description: Neither China nor Japan wants war. The danger is that each side increasingly believes the other is preparing for one.

title: Russian Forces Suffer First Net Territory Loss Since August 2024 as Ukraine Escalates Strike Campaign
summary: ISW assessed that Russian forces lost a net 116 square kilometers of controlled territory in April 2026, the first such net loss since Ukraine's Kursk Oblast incursion in August 2024. Ukrainian drone interdiction campaigns now target Russian logistics near Mariupol.
contents:
## Russian Territorial Reversal in Ukraine

### Historic Shift

The Institute for the Study of War (ISW) assessed that Russian forces in April 2026 suffered a net loss of 116 square kilometers of controlled territory in the Ukrainian theater, marking the first net territorial reversal since Ukraine's August 2024 incursion into Kursk Oblast. The Russian rate of advance has declined steadily since November 2025.

### Declining Russian Momentum

Russian forces averaged 2.9 square kilometers per day in the first four months of 2026, compared with 9.76 square kilometers per day during the same period in 2025. Seasonal weather patterns, including the muddy rasputitsa season, contributed to reduced mechanized movement capability. Ukrainian ground counterattacks, mid-range strikes, and the February 2026 Starlink terminal block in Ukraine compounded existing Russian military problems.

### Ukrainian Drone Interdiction Campaign

Ukrainian Hornet drones now operate freely over the T-0509 Mariupol-Donetsk City highway, targeting Russian tanker trucks and military transport vehicles. Russian milbloggers expressed serious concern that the situation along ground lines of communication (GLOCs) near Mariupol resembles conditions on the M-30 highway where Ukrainian forces have paralyzed logistics. Starlink-enabled drones with ranges up to 200 kilometers reach the M-14 Mariupol-Berdyansk-Melitopol-Henichesk highway, a key Russian route to occupied Crimea.

### Ceasefire Violations

During the May 9-11 Victory Day ceasefire, both sides continued limited offensive operations. Ukrainian forces reported that Russian forces used the ceasefire to pull forward reserves and stockpile Molniya fixed-wing drones. FIRMS satellite data confirmed hostilities decreased but did not cease.

### Putin's Nuclear Posturing

Putin claimed on May 12 that Russian forces successfully tested the RS-28 Sarmat super-heavy ICBM, announcing plans to deploy missiles by the end of 2026. ISW assessed this rhetoric aims to distract from Russia's worsening battlefield performance and the embarrassment of requesting a ceasefire for the Victory Day parade.

title: USNS Robert E. Peary Completes 154-Day Counter-Narcotics Deployment Supporting Operation Southern Spear
summary: Military Sealift Command's USNS Robert E. Peary returned to Norfolk after a 154-day deployment supporting Operation Southern Spear, executing 64 at-sea replenishments and transferring over 19 million gallons of fuel to 17 Navy vessels.
contents:
## MSC Logistics Ship Completes Extended Caribbean Deployment

### Mission Summary

The Lewis and Clark-class dry cargo ship USNS Robert E. Peary (T-AKE 5) and its 105 civil service mariners returned to Naval Station Norfolk on April 29, 2026, concluding a 154-day deployment in the US 4th Fleet area of responsibility. The vessel supported Operation Southern Spear (OSS), a counter-narcotics mission targeting Designated Terrorist Organizations and illicit maritime activity threatening Western Hemisphere stability.

### Operational Achievement

As the sole dry cargo and ammunition vessel in the Caribbean theater, Peary executed 64 at-sea replenishments, transferred 19,488,042 gallons of fuel, performed 16 cargo consolidations at sea (CONSOLS), conducted 13 vertical replenishments (VERTREPS), and handled 12,899 pallets. The ship supplied 17 Navy vessels including the Gerald R. Ford Carrier Strike Group, USS Iwo Jima Amphibious Ready Group, and USS Lake Erie.

### Navy Medicine Modernization

Separately, the 2026 Navy Medicine Logistics Symposium held at Naval Medical Center Portsmouth on May 4 addressed expeditionary medical logistics for contested environments. Leaders focused on sustaining scalable, modular medical systems designed to handle prolonged care and delayed patient movement, recognizing that distributed maritime operations require different approaches to medical capability generation.

### Fleet Readiness

Additional MSC ships — USNS Kanawha, USNS Supply, and tanker Overseas Mykonos — provided supplemental replenishment and logistic services to warfighting ships in the Caribbean alongside Peary. USNS Mercy currently undergoes maintenance in Portland, Oregon, preparing for future missions.

title: China Deploys Research Vessel and Maritime Militia Across Spratly Features for Three-Week Unauthorized Survey
summary: China's Xiang Yang Hong 33 research vessel conducted unauthorized marine scientific research across multiple Philippine-claimed Spratly features over three weeks, supported by over 40 maritime militia vessels, raising concerns across Southeast Asia.
contents:
## Chinese Survey Operations in Contested Spratly Waters

### Unauthorized Research Campaign

China's oceanographic research vessel Xiang Yang Hong 33, capable of supporting submersible craft, conducted an extensive unauthorized survey campaign across the Spratly Island chain from mid-April through early May 2026. The vessel operated systematically across multiple Philippine-claimed features without Manila's consent, violating the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS).

### Movement Pattern

Canada's Dark Vessel Detection System and Philippine Coast Guard air patrols tracked the vessel's movements: Ayungin Shoal (April 20-22), Escoda Shoal (April 22-28), Arellano Reef and Bulig Shoal (April 28-May 1), Panganiban Reef (May 1-3), Quirino Atoll and Rozul Reef (May 3-6), then Jacinto Reef to Diego Silang Reef from May 6 onward.

### Maritime Militia Presence

Philippine Coast Guard detected 41 Chinese maritime militia vessels (CMMVs) anchored near Rozul Reef and Pag-asa Island during the research vessel's operations. Twenty-eight CMMVs clustered around Cay 1 and Cay 2 near Pag-asa Island. PCG Commandant Admiral Ronnie Gil Gavan described the activities as "a clear and blatant violation" of UNCLOS.

### Regional Security Implications

The research operations coincide with Philippine infrastructure upgrades on Thitu Island, where a roughly 200-meter runway extension now proceeds, and Nanshan Island, where a sheltered port expansion has entered early construction phases. These upgrades aim to strengthen Manila's ability to project presence and sustain operations across its Spratly outposts.

### ASEAN Concerns

Vietnam and other ASEAN claimant states have expressed concerns about China's expanding survey activities, which often precede more assertive territorial claims and infrastructure development on disputed features.

title: Dark Vessel Activity Surges 600 Percent in Strait of Hormuz as Maritime Visibility Collapses
summary: Windward Maritime Intelligence reported dark vessel activity near the Strait of Hormuz increased approximately 600 percent between April 19 and May 3, with only 3 of 97 detected vessels transmitting AIS near northern Hormuz corridor.
contents:
## Maritime Visibility Crisis at Strait of Hormuz

### Unprecedented Dark Activity Surge

Windward Maritime Intelligence documented a roughly 600 percent increase in dark vessel activity between April 19 and May 3, 2026, with dark incidents peaking at 671 on May 2 and the seven-day rolling average reaching approximately 470 incidents per day. One month after the ceasefire, the Strait of Hormuz remains operationally unstable.

### Collapse of AIS Visibility

Satellite imagery collected on May 7 revealed substantial vessel movement continuing under dark conditions — five outbound and four inbound dark commercial vessels above 150 meters transiting the northern corridor near Larak Island. AIS-based monitoring showed almost zero visible commercial traffic, yet physical transit continued. On May 5, Windward identified 167 commercial-size vessels, including 146 operating dark. A separate May 6 SAR collection found 97 vessels, with only three transmitting AIS.

### GPS Jamming Operations

Approximately 470 vessels experienced GPS jamming near Fujairah and Khor Fakkan within a 24-hour period, creating additional navigation hazards. Three distinct jamming clusters appeared off Fujairah, complicating commercial operations beyond the already dangerous conditions.

### Continued US Enforcement

US forces intercepted and disabled the Iran-flagged tanker M/T HASNA on May 6 using 20mm cannon fire from an F/A-18 launched from USS Abraham Lincoln after the vessel ignored multiple warnings. The global shadow fleet contracted slightly in April, with 37 removals exceeding 24 additions, bringing the total to 2,793 vessels.

### Iranian Oil Routes Shift

Iran-linked routing increasingly shifted toward Lombok and Sunda Strait passages to avoid visibility around the Malacca Strait, while ship-to-ship transfers continued near Malaysian waters with at least 42 transfers tracked by satellite imagery.

link_preview.title: One Month Into the Ceasefire, Dark Shipping Dominates Hormuz
link_preview.description: One month into the ceasefire, AIS suppression, GPS jamming, dark tanker activity, and covert transits through Hormuz continue disrupting maritime visibility.

title: Japan Emerges as Indo-Pacific Middle Power Arms Supplier with Frigate Exports to Australia and New Zealand
summary: Japan's emerging defense export strategy centers on the upgraded Mogami-class frigate platform selected by Australia and under consideration by New Zealand, potentially creating a shared 37-ship fleet across three Indo-Pacific navies.
contents:
## Japan's Middle Power Defense Export Strategy

### Arms Export Revolution

Japan's April 2026 Cabinet decision removed long-standing restrictions on lethal weapons exports, opening the door for Tokyo to serve as anchor of a shared defense industrial ecosystem among Indo-Pacific middle powers. The move goes beyond the previous "five categories" limitation that confined defense exports to rescue, transport, warning, surveillance, and minesweeping equipment.

### Mogami-Class Frigate Network

Australia selected Japan's upgraded Mogami-class frigate (06FFM or "New FFM") as its future general-purpose frigate platform, planning 11 vessels with the first three built in Japan. New Zealand identified the same frigate as a finalist in its replacement program on May 7. If selected, Japan would operate 12 Mogami-class and 12 New FFMs, Australia would field 11, and New Zealand at least two — creating over 37 ships across three navies sharing a common platform family.

### Philippines Partnership

Tokyo and Manila agreed in May 2026 to establish a working group for potential transfer of Abukuma-class destroyer escorts, which could represent Japan's first export of lethal military equipment under the revised framework. This builds on the Reciprocal Access Agreement and Acquisition and Cross-Servicing Agreement signed earlier.

### Strategic Rationale

Japanese policymakers recognize that Washington's long-term strategic reliability cannot remain guaranteed, sometimes referred to privately as the "Trump risk." The defense export strategy creates horizontal ties among regional middle powers, building resilience complementing rather than replacing the US alliance system. Indonesia signed a Defense Cooperation Agreement with Tokyo in May 2026.

### China's Two-Front Challenge

The expanding Japan-Philippines defense cooperation stitches together the East China Sea and South China Sea into a connected theater. This operational integration — supported by the RAA, Official Security Assistance, and ACSA mechanisms — complicates China's ability to compartmentalize its maritime security fronts and manage tensions separately with Japan and the Philippines.

link_preview.title: Japan’s Middle Power Arms Strategy in the Indo-Pacific
link_preview.description: Tokyo’s defense exports are becoming tools for building a new middle power security network across the Indo-Pacific.

title: Sweden Seizes Shadow Fleet Tanker Jin Hui in Fifth Baltic Intervention as European Enforcement Accelerates
summary: Swedish Coast Guard boarded and detained the sanctioned tanker Jin Hui near Trelleborg on May 3, arresting the Chinese captain for false flag documentation. The seizure marked Sweden's fifth shadow fleet intervention in recent weeks.
contents:
## Swedish Shadow Fleet Enforcement in the Baltic

### Fifth Intervention

The Swedish Coast Guard and police jointly boarded and detained the tanker Jin Hui in Swedish territorial waters south of Trelleborg on May 3, 2026. The operation represented Sweden's fifth shadow fleet intervention in recent weeks, following actions against Sea Owl I, Caffa, Flora 1, and Hui Yuan. The Chinese captain received arrest on suspicion of using false documents and violating seaworthiness laws.

### False Flag Pattern

The 183-meter oil/chemical tanker sailed under a Syrian flag, but Equasis records confirmed the registration appeared fraudulent — Syria's maritime authority notified the International Maritime Organization that it did not register the vessel. Under UNCLOS, ships unable to establish genuine flag state links have stateless status, granting coastal states boarding authority. The Jin Hui appeared on European Union, United Kingdom, and Ukrainian sanctions lists.

### Coordinated Evasion Network

Windward Maritime Intelligence identified that on February 27, 2026 — the same day Jin Hui adopted the Syrian flag — another sanctioned shadow fleet tanker, the Lotus (IMO 9392822), also began broadcasting Syria as its flag via AIS. A third vessel, the Aegis (IMO 9275660), also flies a Syrian flag. The simultaneous adoption suggests coordinated management or a shared facilitator advising sanctioned fleet operators.

### European Enforcement Surge

Vessel interdictions surged 160 percent in Q1 2026 compared to Q4 2025, with 92 percent of interdicted vessels sanctioned, 85 percent falsely flagged, and 12 of 13 cases involving tankers. Belgium, France, and Sweden led European enforcement actions. The shadow fleet totals approximately 2,793 vessels globally, with Russia remaining the dominant crude origin at 67 million barrels monthly.

### Russia Responds with Naval Escorts

Russian military vessels have begun escorting shadow fleet tankers through the English Channel and Baltic Sea, representing a concerning escalation in which a state navy protects rule-breaking vessels through waters of coastal states enforcing maritime rules.

link_preview.title: Sweden detains sanctioned oil tanker believed to be linked to Russia's shadow fleet
link_preview.description: Sweden's coast guard has detained a tanker suspected of being part of Russia’s so-called “shadow fleet.”

title: US Treasury Sanctions IRGC Oil Network Enabling Iran-China Crude Trade Under Economic Fury Campaign
summary: OFAC designated 12 individuals and entities for facilitating IRGC oil sales to China, targeting the Shahid Purja'fari Oil Headquarters network. The action continues the Economic Fury campaign amid the US naval blockade of Iranian ports.
contents:
## OFAC Targets IRGC Oil Sales Network

### Designation Details

The US Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) designated 12 individuals and entities on May 11 for enabling the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps' sale and shipment of Iranian oil to China. Three Iranian nationals received designations, including the chief of IRGC Shahid Purja'fari Oil Headquarters, its financial head, and the commercial chief.

### Targeted Entities

Among the sanctioned entities, four operate from the United Arab Emirates, four from Hong Kong, and one from Oman. Key targets include Hong Kong Blue Ocean Limited and Hong Kong Sanmu Limited, both accused of arranging the sale and shipment of Iranian oil to overseas buyers. The designations also targeted Zeus Logistics Group in Oman and Ocean Allianz Shipping LLC in the UAE.

### Economic Fury Campaign

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent stated the department will continue depriving the Iranian regime of funding for weapons programs, terrorist proxies, and nuclear ambitions. The action represents part of the broader "Economic Fury" campaign launched on April 15 to obstruct Iran's alternate revenue streams during the ongoing US-imposed blockade of Iranian ports.

### Broader Sanctions Context

The designation followed earlier OFAC actions in May targeting Iranian shadow banking networks, weapons procurement facilitators in China and Belarus, and shipping companies operating in Iran's petroleum sector. The State Department's Rewards for Justice program simultaneously offered up to $15 million for information leading to disruption of IRGC financial mechanisms.

### Strategic Significance

The sanctions reinforce maximum economic pressure on Tehran amid a stalemate in war-ending negotiations following the February 28 US-Israeli strikes on Iran and the subsequent April ceasefire. The US naval blockade, imposed on April 13, has already reduced Iranian oil loadings by more than half.

title: AFP Monitors 35 Chinese Naval and Coast Guard Vessels Across West Philippine Sea Features
summary: The Armed Forces of the Philippines tracked 35 Chinese vessels including PLAN warships and Coast Guard ships across Scarborough Shoal, Ayungin Shoal, Escoda Shoal, and Pag-asa Island from May 4-11, continuing aggressive maritime presence.
contents:
## Chinese Military Presence in West Philippine Sea

### Weekly Monitoring Report

The Armed Forces of the Philippines monitored 35 Chinese vessels across four key West Philippine Sea features from May 4 to 11, 2026. AFP spokesperson Rear Admiral Roy Vincent Trinidad reported the deployment includes both People's Liberation Army Navy (PLAN) warships and China Coast Guard (CCG) vessels.

### Distribution by Feature

Scarborough Shoal (Bajo de Masinloc) recorded the heaviest Chinese presence with 17 vessels — eight PLAN warships and nine CCG vessels. The shoal, approximately 220 kilometers from Luzon, has remained under effective Chinese control since a 2012 standoff. Ayungin Shoal hosted seven vessels (one PLAN, six CCG), Escoda Shoal tracked five vessels (three PLAN, two CCG), and Pag-asa Island recorded six vessels (three PLAN, three CCG).

### Chinese Research Vessel Operations

The Philippine Coast Guard simultaneously detected the oceanographic research vessel Xiang Yang Hong 33 conducting unauthorized marine scientific research across multiple features within the Philippines' exclusive economic zone. The vessel moved through Ayungin Shoal, Escoda Shoal, Panganiban Reef, Quirino Atoll, and Rozul Reef over a three-week period.

### Filipino Sovereignty Assertions

The Atin Ito coalition planted a Philippine flag on Sandy Cay 1 during its fourth civilian mission to the West Philippine Sea on May 3, despite the visible presence of Chinese Coast Guard vessels. Representative Dadah Kiram Ismula and student leader Matthew Christian Silverio conducted the landing despite attempted pursuit by Chinese boats.

### Infrastructure Upgrades

Satellite imagery confirmed ongoing Philippine construction on Thitu Island, extending its runway by approximately 200 meters to accommodate military aircraft, and port expansion on Nanshan Island to strengthen enforcement operations.

title: North Korea Tests Tactical Ballistic Missile With New Autonomous Guidance System on May 17
summary: North Korea's Missile General Bureau conducted a test launch of a tactical ballistic missile equipped with a new autonomous navigation system on May 17. South Korea's Joint Chiefs of Staff confirmed the missiles flew approximately 300 kilometers into the Sea of Japan.
contents:
## North Korea Tactical Ballistic Missile Test

### Test Launch Details
The North Korean Missile General Bureau conducted a test launch of a tactical ballistic missile equipped with a new navigation system of autonomous guidance on May 17. The Korean Central News Agency (KCNA) confirmed the test verified the accuracy and reliability of the autonomous guidance system. North Korean leader Kim Jong Un personally supervised the launch.

### South Korean and Allied Response
South Korea's Joint Chiefs of Staff reported that North Korea launched several short-range ballistic missiles into the Sea of Japan on May 17, with the projectiles traveling approximately 300 kilometers. The test launch formed part of what KCNA described as regular activities by the Missile General Bureau and subordinate defense research institutes for active weapons technology development.

### Strategic Context
This test follows an intensified series of missile launches since the Ninth Party Congress in February 2026, which committed Pyongyang to building more nuclear weapons and delivery systems. The new autonomous guidance system potentially eliminates reliance on GPS or external navigation updates, making the missiles more resistant to electronic warfare countermeasures. Kim Jong Un has continued pressing forward with weapons modernization efforts, including recent inspections of munitions factories where he called for strengthening mortar and howitzer forces.

title: Kim Jong Un Calls for Expanded Mortar and Howitzer Production During Munitions Factory Inspections
summary: Kim Jong Un visited multiple munitions industrial enterprises to review first-half 2026 weapons production plans, ordering establishment of a specialized artillery production complex and calling for strengthened mortar and howitzer forces.
contents:
## Kim Jong Un Munitions Factory Inspections

### Production Review
North Korean leader Kim Jong Un visited several munitions industrial enterprises on May 11 to review the implementation of military production tasks for the first half of 2026, according to the Korean Central News Agency. The visit represented part of an intensified military inspection campaign by Kim in recent days.

### Artillery Expansion Orders
Kim repeatedly stressed the need to strengthen mortar and howitzer forces during his factory visits. He ordered the establishment of a specialized artillery production complex and a small-arms production factory to meet future military demands. At a bullet factory, Kim called for a production system of high-precision multipurpose bullets of various calibers, as well as special-function and training rounds.

### Recent Military Activities
The inspection followed Kim's May 7 visit to the newly built 5,000-ton destroyer Choe Hyon, where he observed a maneuvering assessment test ahead of commissioning. A day earlier, during a visit to a major munitions factory, Kim revealed plans to deploy a new 155-millimeter self-propelled gun-howitzer system capable of placing Seoul within range along the southern border by end of this year. KCNA reported workers in the munitions sector had achieved record growth in shell and bullet production.

title: US Allows Russian Oil Sanctions Waiver to Expire Amid Iran War Maritime Disruptions
summary: The Trump administration allowed a controversial waiver on sanctions targeting Russian seaborne oil to expire on May 16, reimposing restrictions that had temporarily enabled countries to purchase Russian crude during the Iran conflict.
contents:
## Russian Oil Sanctions Waiver Expiration

### Policy Change
The Trump administration allowed General License 134B to expire on May 16, reimposing restrictions on Russian seaborne oil transactions. The Treasury Department declined to renew the waiver, which had permitted transactions involving Russian oil cargoes already loaded onto tankers before sanctions deadlines took effect.

### Background and Context
The waiver originated in March 2026 as a temporary safeguard to prevent a deeper global energy shock. Administration officials defended the measure as necessary while fighting in the Middle East pushed oil prices higher and threatened key shipping routes through the Strait of Hormuz. The waiver received one extension in April before its final expiration.

### Implications for Maritime Trade
The expiration highlights the increasingly difficult balance Washington faces between tightening pressure on Moscow and preventing further disruption to global energy markets already rattled by the Iran conflict. Countries such as India, which had continued purchasing Russian crude under the waiver, now face renewed restrictions. The decision intersects with ongoing US naval blockade enforcement against Iranian ports, where CENTCOM has redirected 75 commercial vessels since the blockade took effect. Russia's shadow fleet continues adapting through dark shipping, AIS spoofing, and alternative routing to circumvent sanctions enforcement.

title: Hezbollah Fiber-Optic FPV Drones Pose Unprecedented Threat to Israeli Forces in Southern Lebanon
summary: BBC Verify geolocated 35 Hezbollah FPV drone strike videos since March 26, documenting a tactical shift to fiber-optic guided drones that bypass Israeli electronic countermeasures. The IDF has deployed 158,000 square meters of mesh netting as interim protection.
contents:
## Hezbollah FPV Drone Tactical Evolution

### Fiber-Optic Innovation
Hezbollah has dramatically increased its use of small first-person view drones against Israeli targets, including systems controlled by fiber-optic cables that render Israeli electronic warfare capabilities largely irrelevant. BBC Verify geolocated 35 strike videos shared by Hezbollah since March 26, documenting attacks on Israeli soldiers, armored vehicles, and air defense systems in southern Lebanon and northern Israel.

### Equipment and Casualties
The IDF confirmed four soldiers and one civilian have died from FPV strikes, with dozens more injured. One verified video from May 15 showed at least four FPV drones attacking an Israeli border outpost near Kiryat Shmona, targeting military vehicles in sequence. At least two vehicles sustained heavy damage or destruction. Israel's Security Cabinet decided to establish a dedicated IDF factory producing thousands of FPV suicide drones monthly, staffed entirely by Haredi soldiers.

### IDF Countermeasures
The IDF Ground Forces commander established seven dedicated task forces in late April to address the threat. Solutions under development include computer-vision fire control sights for assault rifles, 5.56mm fragmenting ammunition ordered from the United States, and defensive mesh netting. Approximately 158,000 square meters of netting have reached troops, with an additional 188,000 square meters procurement underway. Dr. Andreas Krieg of King's College London noted that fiber-optic connections make Israel's capacity to detect, jam, and intercept drones largely irrelevant, costing only $300-$500 per drone.

title: Iran Crude Oil Exports Halted for Over 28 Days Under US Naval Blockade Pressure
summary: Iran has not successfully exported any crude oil by sea for 28 days as the US naval blockade imposed in April continues restricting maritime trade. Loading at Kharg Island remains disrupted following a suspected oil spill near the terminal.
contents:
## Iran Crude Export Blockade Update

### Export Halt Confirmed
According to TankerTrackers ship-tracking data, Iran has not successfully exported any crude oil by sea for over 28 days. The monitoring firm defines a successful export as a tanker crossing the US Navy blockade line without returning with its cargo. Some refined petroleum products have still managed to leave Iran because the US Treasury's OFAC has not sanctioned the tankers involved.

### Kharg Island Disruption
Kharg Island, the hub for 90 percent of Iran's oil exports, has not loaded any tankers since May 6 due to an oil leak that Tehran denied. Satellite images showed the suspected oil spill spreading across approximately 45 square kilometers of water near the island between May 6 and May 8. Multiple empty and loaded tankers remain clustered near the blockade perimeter and in waters off Pakistan.

### Blockade Enforcement
US Central Command has redirected 75 commercial vessels and disabled four since the blockade began on April 13. The blockade applies to all ships heading into or from Iranian ports. Meanwhile, Iran-linked tankers continue adapting by taking alternative routes through the Lombok and Sunda straits to avoid detection in the Malacca Strait, while dark fleet activity near Bandar Abbas and Chabahar persists with only 25 percent of detected vessels transmitting AIS signals.

title: Iran Plans Stock Market Reopening After Wartime Suspension as Nuclear Talks Stall
summary: Iran announced plans to reopen its stock market on May 19 after suspending trading during the active war phase with the US and Israel. The reopening comes as nuclear deal negotiations remain stalled, with Trump rejecting Iran's latest proposal.
contents:
## Iran Stock Market Reopening and Nuclear Talks

### Market Reopening
Iran plans to reopen its stock market on May 19 after the suspension imposed during the active phase of the war with the United States and Israel, according to Iran's IRNA news agency. Hamid Yari of Iran's Securities and Exchange Organization stated that the suspension aimed at protecting shareholders' assets, preventing panic-driven trading, and allowing more transparent pricing conditions.

### Nuclear Negotiations Deadlock
President Trump reviewed Iran's latest agreement offer but rejected it, stating that any deal containing nuclear enrichment provisions remains unacceptable. Trump indicated that a 20-year suspension of enrichment might suffice but the level of guarantee from Tehran remains insufficient. The diplomatic stalemate continues as the ceasefire with Iran holds but tensions persist over sanctions, regional security, and the nuclear program.

### Domestic Economic Impact
The war has devastated Iran's economy across multiple sectors. Iranian handwoven carpet exports, once generating $2.5 billion annually, have virtually stopped. Approximately 30 people have faced execution on political grounds since the conflict began on February 28. Fuel shortages and rationing have pushed Iranians into a gasoline black market. The stock market reopening represents a tentative signal of economic normalization, though the ongoing naval blockade continues choking oil revenues that fund government operations.

title: US Extends Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire by 45 Days as Pentagon Opens Security Track Talks
summary: The United States extended the ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon by 45 days and announced a new security track at the Pentagon for military coordination. Fighting between the IDF and Hezbollah continues despite the diplomatic progress.
contents:
## Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire Extension

### Diplomatic Development
The US State Department announced a 45-day extension of the ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon following the fourth round of talks between Israeli and Lebanese representatives in Washington. The announcement came at the conclusion of talks that included higher-level envoys from both countries, with Lebanon represented by attorney Simon Karam and Israel by Deputy National Security Adviser Yossi Draznin.

### Security Track Opening
For the first time, a security track will open at the Pentagon on May 29 with military delegations from both countries. US officials indicated they seek to create military coordination between Israel and Lebanon similar to what currently exists with the Palestinian Authority. Additional diplomatic talks will resume at the State Department on June 2 and 3.

### Continued Fighting
Despite the ceasefire extension, intense combat continues. The IDF struck approximately 100 targets in southern Lebanon over the weekend, including observation posts, terror infrastructure, and a Hezbollah weapons depot. Captain Maoz Israel Recanati, 24, a platoon commander in the Golani Brigade, died from an FPV drone strike in southern Lebanon. Hezbollah launched FPV drones and mortar fire at Israeli positions, while also conducting cross-border attacks that triggered alerts across the Western Galilee. Lebanon's Health Ministry reports 2,896 deaths since the conflict began on March 2, with around 400 occurring since the nominal ceasefire.

title: Dark Shipping Activity Surges 600 Percent in Strait of Hormuz as Maritime Visibility Collapses
summary: Windward Maritime AI detected a 600 percent increase in dark vessel activity through the Strait of Hormuz between April 19 and May 3, with satellite imagery revealing substantial vessel movement invisible to standard AIS tracking systems.
contents:
## Hormuz Maritime Visibility Crisis

### Dark Activity Surge
Windward Maritime AI analysis detected a dramatic 600 percent increase in dark vessel activity in the Strait of Hormuz between April 19 and May 3, with dark incidents peaking at 671 on May 2. The seven-day rolling average reached approximately 470 incidents per day. AIS-visible commercial traffic dropped sharply while satellite imagery increasingly revealed substantial vessel movement continuing entirely under dark conditions.

### Geospatial Detection Findings
Satellite imagery collected on May 7 over the northern Hormuz corridor near Larak Island identified five likely outbound dark commercial vessels and four likely inbound dark commercial vessels above 150 meters. The outbound group included one VLCC, one large container vessel, and three oil or chemical tankers. A separate May 6 collection identified 97 vessels in the broader operating area, with 84 operating without AIS transmission.

### GPS Jamming Operations
Approximately 470 vessels experienced GPS jamming near Fujairah and Khor Fakkan within a single 24-hour window. Three distinct jamming clusters appeared off Fujairah. The combination of AIS suppression, GPS interference, and dark fleet operations has created an environment where commercial vessel activity has become increasingly disconnected from traditional visibility frameworks, forcing operators to rely exclusively on satellite-based monitoring for operational awareness.

title: Iran-Linked LPG Tanker Crosses US Navy Blockade Line as Ghost Fleet Adapts Routes
summary: An Iran-linked LPG carrier Tara Gas crossed the formal US Navy blockade line and sailed into the Arabian Sea, while UANI tracking identified at least 37 oil-laden tankers operating inside the Persian Gulf west of the Strait of Hormuz.
contents:
## Iran Ghost Fleet Maritime Operations

### Blockade Breach
The very large gas carrier Tara Gas, previously linked to Iranian LPG cargoes, crossed the formal US blockade line stretching from Oman's Ras al Hadd to the Iran-Pakistan border on May 13 and continued sailing southeastward into the Arabian Sea. The vessel had lifted Iranian liquefied petroleum gas in early May according to assessments from ship-tracking platforms Kpler and Vortexa. The vessel's owner, Global Gas Inc., operates from the United Arab Emirates.

### Ghost Fleet Scale
United Against Nuclear Iran (UANI) tracking identified at least 37 tankers laden with Iranian oil operating inside the Persian Gulf. Since the conflict began on February 28, UANI has tracked at least 70 Iranian oil loadings totaling approximately 82 million barrels, generating an estimated $6 billion in revenue for the IRGC. At least 32 Iran-flagged tankers have reached Southeast Asian waters bound for Malaysia's Eastern Outer Port Limits anchorage since the war started.

### Alternative Routing
Iran-flagged tankers HUGE and DERYA adopted uneconomical routes through Indonesia's Lombok Strait rather than the traditional Malacca and Singapore straits to avoid US naval interdiction. Both vessels briefly broadcast AIS while transiting Lombok. CENTCOM has redirected 65 commercial vessels and disabled four since the blockade began, but the continuing flow of Iranian cargoes through alternative routes demonstrates the adaptive capacity of Iran's maritime logistics network.

title: Hezbollah Fires Approximately 200 Projectiles at Israel as IDF Strikes 100 Targets in Southern Lebanon
summary: Hezbollah launched approximately 200 projectiles at Israel and its troops over the weekend, violating the ceasefire extension. The IDF responded by striking 100 Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon including command centers and weapons depots.
contents:
## Israel-Hezbollah Escalation Despite Ceasefire

### Hezbollah Attacks
Hezbollah launched approximately 200 projectiles against Israel and Israeli troops over the weekend, directly violating the 45-day ceasefire extension agreed upon on May 16. The attacks included FPV drones, mortar fire, and rocket barrages targeting Israeli forces in southern Lebanon and civilian communities in northern Israel. At 15:38 UTC on May 17, an alert sounded in Metula and one UAV received interception. Additional alerts activated in western Galilee communities throughout the day.

### IDF Response
The IDF struck 100 targets in southern Lebanon over the weekend, including observation posts, terror infrastructure, and a Hezbollah weapons depot. In the al-Khoush area near Tyre, a smoke plume appeared after strikes not far from civilian establishments. Israeli forces continued clearing areas south of the Litani River while maintaining operations against Hezbollah military infrastructure.

### Casualties and Diplomatic Context
Captain Maoz Israel Recanati, 24, a platoon commander in the Golani Brigade's 12th Battalion, died from an FPV drone strike in southern Lebanon. Prime Minister Netanyahu directed his team to find solutions for the FPV drone threat. Since the conflict began on March 2, Lebanon reports 2,896 deaths including over 400 since the ceasefire. Twenty Israeli soldiers, two civilians, and one defense contractor have also died. Hezbollah declared the Lebanon-Israel talks a dead-end and rejected external diplomatic pressure.

title: Drone Strike Causes Fire at UAE Barakah Nuclear Power Plant Amid Iran War Deadlock
summary: A drone strike hit an electrical generator outside the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant in the United Arab Emirates on May 17, prompting emergency diesel generators to power the facility. The IAEA called for maximum military restraint near nuclear installations as the US-Iran diplomatic deadlock persists.
contents:
## Drone Strike on Barakah Nuclear Plant

### Attack Details

A drone struck an electrical generator outside the inner perimeter of the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant in Abu Dhabi on Sunday, May 17. The Abu Dhabi Media Office confirmed that radiological safety levels remained unaffected and no injuries occurred. Emergency diesel generators began providing power to the plant's unit 3 following the incident.

### UAE Response and Attribution

Emirati officials stated they have launched an investigation into the source of the strike and affirmed the UAE's full right to respond to such "terrorist attacks." The UAE defense ministry reported that two additional drones "successfully" neutralized and that the drones had originated from the "western border," though it provided no further elaboration.

### International Reaction

The International Atomic Energy Agency confirmed it closely monitors the situation and called for "maximum military restraint" near any nuclear power plant. This attack represents the latest in a series of Iranian strikes targeting UAE energy infrastructure during the ongoing US-Israeli conflict with Iran that began on February 28.

### Diplomatic Stalemate

More than five weeks after a tenuous ceasefire took effect, US and Iranian demands remain far apart. Washington demands Tehran dismantle its nuclear program and lift its hold on the Strait of Hormuz. Iran demands compensation for war damage, an end to the US blockade of Iranian ports, and a halt to fighting on all fronts, including in Lebanon. The disruption to shipping through the Strait of Hormuz has caused the most significant oil supply crisis in history, pushing up global energy prices.

### Regional Context

Iran stepped up attacks on the UAE earlier this month after US President Donald Trump announced a naval mission to open the Strait of Hormuz, which he suspended after 48 hours. A senior Iranian armed forces spokesperson warned on Sunday that if Trump's threats materialized, the US would "face new, aggressive, and surprise scenarios."

link_preview.title: UAE reports drone strike at nuclear power plant as Iran war deadlock endures
link_preview.description: DUBAI, May 17 (Reuters) - A drone strike caused a fire at a nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates, officials in Abu Dhabi said on Sunday, at a time when progress appears to have stalled in efforts to end the U.-Israeli war with Iran and restart shipping in the Gulf.

title: Philippines Ranked Among Most Cyber-Attacked Nations Globally as ASEAN Identity Threats Surge
summary: A new IBM X-Force Threat Intelligence Index identifies the Philippines alongside Indonesia, Thailand, and South Korea as the most attacked countries globally. Organizations across ASEAN face expanding identity-based attack surfaces driven by AI-enabled cybercrime, with 68 percent of regional firms at only intermediate cybersecurity maturity.
contents:
## Philippines Cybersecurity Threat Landscape

### Global Attack Ranking

The 2025 IBM X-Force Threat Intelligence Index has identified the Philippines as one of the most cyber-attacked countries globally, ranking alongside Indonesia, Thailand, and South Korea. This ranking reinforces the region's position as a high-intensity threat landscape where digital acceleration outpaces defensive measures.

### Identity-Based Attack Surface

Digital acceleration across ASEAN has created a larger and more fluid identity attack surface than most organizations can fully account for. Every new application, API integration, service account, or AI agent introduces a new identity, many existing outside the scope of traditional identity and access management systems. Non-human identities and AI agents proliferate faster and act more autonomously yet face governance with significantly less rigor.

### AI-Enabled Cybercrime Escalation

Fortinet's research presented at the Accelerate APAC Philippines Edition 2026 revealed that 57 percent of organizations now consider AI-driven threats their top cybersecurity risk. Alert overload affects 50 percent of security teams, while many companies juggle 20 to 40 separate security tools. Ransomware victims surged 389 percent year-over-year, reaching 7,831 confirmed victims globally.

### Philippine-South Korea Cybersecurity Partnership

The Philippines and South Korea signed a $25.6 million agreement to establish a National Cyber Security Center, the largest KOICA-funded project in the Philippines to date. The five-year initiative will serve as the government's main hub for monitoring cyber threats, responding to incidents, and coordinating national cybersecurity efforts.

### Regulatory Pressure

New regulatory developments across ASEAN increase pressure on organizations to control access with clarity and context. Vietnam's Personal Data Protection Law, Brunei's data protection order, and Myanmar's Cybersecurity Law 1/2025 all introduce legal consequences for unauthorized access and mishandling of sensitive data.

title: Japan Establishes Pacific Defense Architecture Office to Counter China and North Korea Threats
summary: Japan's Ministry of Defense established the Pacific Defense Architecture Office on April 1, 2026, marking a shift from solely countering North Korean missiles to addressing China's expanded military operations beyond the First Island Chain. Mobile radar deployment to Kita-Daito Island and port development on Iwo Jima accelerate as part of the new strategy.
contents:
## Japan's Pacific Defense Pivot

### New Defense Office

The Japanese Ministry of Defense established the Pacific Defense Architecture Office on April 1, 2026, signaling Japan's intention to strengthen its defensive posture in the Pacific region. Until recently, Japan's security policy primarily focused on countering North Korean missile threats and responding to Chinese activities around the Southwest Islands.

### China's Pacific Expansion

China has expanded its influence beyond Japan's island chain into the Pacific Ocean while significantly intensifying military activities. In June 2025, two Chinese aircraft carriers deployed simultaneously to the Pacific for the first time, conducting approximately 1,000 aircraft takeoff and landing exercises. This deployment marked the first instance of a Chinese carrier advancing east of the Second Island Chain, operating within Japan's exclusive economic zone near Minamitorishima.

### Concrete Defense Measures

Japan plans to accelerate deployment of a mobile air traffic control radar to Kita-Daito Island, approximately 360 kilometers east of Okinawa. The phased-array radar system, the same technology used aboard Aegis-equipped destroyers, enables continuous 360-degree surveillance. Surveys for port facility development on Iwo Jima will also begin this year to enhance Self-Defense Forces transport and logistical capabilities.

### North Korean Threat Remains

North Korea announced plans to deploy new 155mm self-propelled gun-howitzers with striking ranges exceeding 60 kilometers along the southern border, placing Seoul within firing range. Leader Kim Jong Un also ordered the commissioning of the 5,000-ton destroyer Choe Hyon by mid-June, continuing Pyongyang's naval modernization push.

### Alliance Implications

Japan's Pacific defense strengthening complements US military capabilities and contributes significantly to enhancing overall deterrence of the Japan-US alliance. Defense Minister Koizumi stated that strengthening the defense system across the vast Pacific sea and airspace remains "an urgent issue" and that "the current posture remains not necessarily sufficient."

link_preview.title: Japan's efforts to strengthen its Pacific defense - Asia Pacific Security Magazine
link_preview.description: The Japanese Ministry of Defense established thePacific Defense Architecture Officeon April 1, 2026, underscoring Japan’s intention to strengthen its defensive posture in the Pacific region

title: Taiwan Detects Eight Chinese Naval Vessels as Trump Holds Arms Deal in Abeyance After Xi Summit
summary: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense detected eight PLA Navy vessels and one official ship operating around the island on May 17, one day after Trump concluded his Beijing summit where he described the $14 billion Taiwan arms package as a 'very good negotiating chip' and held the deal 'in abeyance.'
contents:
## Post-Summit Cross-Strait Developments

### Chinese Naval Activity

Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense reported on May 17 that it detected eight People's Liberation Army Navy vessels and one official ship operating around the island, continuing the pattern of elevated Chinese military presence near Taiwan. The ministry stated that ROC Armed Forces monitored the situation and responded accordingly. A day earlier, Taiwan had reported seven Chinese naval vessels and one official ship near its territorial waters.

### Trump-Xi Summit Impact on Taiwan

US President Donald Trump wrapped up a three-day state visit to Beijing on May 16 where Chinese President Xi Jinping pressed him extensively on Taiwan, warning that mishandling the issue could push US-China relations to a "dangerous place." Trump told Fox News afterward that he held the $14 billion Congressional-approved arms package to Taiwan "in abeyance," describing it as "a very good negotiating chip" in dealings with Beijing.

### Arms Sale Uncertainty

The Trump administration approved a record $11 billion arms sale package for Taiwan in December, and Congress approved an additional $14 billion package in January. However, Trump now conditions forward movement on China's behavior, marking a departure from the 1982 Six Assurances that committed Washington not to consult Beijing on arms sales to Taipei. When reminded of those assurances, Trump noted that the 1980s represented "a long way."

### Beijing's Strategic Assessment

Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi stated after the summit that the US side "understands China's position and attaches importance to China's concerns" and "does not support or accept Taiwan moving toward independence." Beijing announced a new era of "constructive strategic stability" between the two powers. Xi accepted Trump's invitation to visit the United States in the fall.

### CSIS China Power Analysis

CSIS data shows the PLA conducted a record 3,764 air incursions into Taiwan's ADIZ in 2025, a 22.4 percent increase from 2024. Average monthly PLAN vessel presence around Taiwan reached 221 since May 2024, a 42 percent increase from the prior period. China conducted two large-scale military exercises around Taiwan in 2025, named Strait Thunder-2025A and Justice Mission 2025.

title: WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in Congo and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern
summary: WHO Director-General Tedros declared the Bundibugyo Ebola virus outbreak a PHEIC on May 17 after 336 suspected cases, 88 deaths across DRC and Uganda, with confirmed cases reaching Kampala and Kinshasa. No approved vaccines or therapeutics exist for this rare Ebola strain.
contents:
## WHO Ebola Emergency Declaration

### Declaration Details

WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus declared the Ebola disease outbreak caused by the Bundibugyo virus in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern (PHEIC) on May 17, 2026. The declaration falls short of a pandemic classification but represents the organization's second-highest alert level under international health regulations.

### Outbreak Scale

As of May 16, the outbreak has produced eight laboratory-confirmed cases, 246 suspected cases, and 80 suspected deaths across at least three health zones in Ituri Province, including Bunia, Rwampara, and Mongbwalu. Two confirmed cases appeared in Kampala, Uganda within 24 hours of each other on May 15-16 among individuals traveling from the DRC. A confirmed case also surfaced in Kinshasa, approximately 1,000 kilometers from the epicenter.

### No Approved Treatments

Unlike previous Ebola-Zaire outbreaks, no approved Bundibugyo virus-specific therapeutics or vaccines currently exist. The Bundibugyo strain has appeared only twice before: Uganda's Bundibugyo district in 2007-2008 (149 cases, 37 deaths) and Isiro, Congo in 2012 (57 cases, 29 deaths). This absence of medical countermeasures significantly complicates the response.

### Compounding Factors

Ongoing insecurity, humanitarian crisis, high population mobility, and the urban nature of the current hotspot all compound the risk of spread. At least four healthcare worker deaths from Ebola symptoms have raised concerns about healthcare-associated transmission and gaps in infection prevention and control measures. Africa CDC Director-General Jean Kaseya noted that 50 deaths occurred before health authorities received their first alert via social media on May 5.

### WHO Guidance

The WHO advised against closure of international borders, noting that restrictions could encourage unmonitored informal crossings. Neighboring countries should activate emergency management systems, strengthen cross-border screening, and isolate confirmed cases immediately. Exposed individuals should avoid international travel for 21 days.

title: North Korea Deploys New Long-Range Artillery Targeting Seoul as Nuclear Arsenal Outgrows US Defenses
summary: North Korea announced deployment of new 155mm self-propelled howitzers with 60-kilometer range along the southern border, placing Seoul within striking distance. Bloomberg analysis indicates Pyongyang's nuclear arsenal now approaches the capacity to overwhelm US ground-based missile defenses, with an estimated 24 ICBMs capable of reaching the US homeland.
contents:
## Korean Peninsula Security Assessment

### New Artillery Deployment

North Korea announced plans to deploy new 155mm self-propelled gun-howitzers along the border with South Korea by year's end. Leader Kim Jong Un inspected production at a munitions factory, confirming the weapons' striking range exceeds 60 kilometers, sufficient to place Seoul and its 10 million residents within firing range from front-line positions. Kim stated that "various operational and tactical missile systems and powerful multiple rocket launcher systems" would also join border deployments.

### Nuclear Arsenal Expansion

A Bloomberg analysis published April 28 revealed that North Korea's nuclear arsenal approaches a crucial tipping point: potentially large enough to overwhelm the US ground-based missile defense system, which cost approximately $65 billion to develop. Analysts estimate Pyongyang could possess as many as 24 ICBMs, with the US GMD system's 44 interceptors requiring at least two launches per target, meaning defenses could face exhaustion against a two-dozen ICBM attack.

### Naval Modernization

Kim ordered the commissioning of the 5,000-ton destroyer Choe Hyon by mid-June after personally observing its maneuvering assessment test. The vessel represents North Korea's largest and most advanced warship, with strategic cruise missiles tested from the platform believed capable of carrying nuclear warheads. Kim has called for construction of two additional destroyers of the same class.

### Constitutional Changes

South Korea confirmed that North Korea's newly revised constitution drops all references to Korean unification, redefining its territory as only the northern half of the peninsula. The changes align with Kim's declaration of South Korea as his country's "permanent and most hostile enemy" since diplomacy stalled following the collapse of Trump-Kim negotiations in 2019.

### Regional Defense Response

South Korea deployed five reconnaissance satellites to strengthen its preemptive strike capabilities. Japan deployed its first battery of upgraded Type-12 antiship missiles with 1,000-kilometer range to Camp Kengun in Kumamoto prefecture, marking the operational activation of Tokyo's counterstrike capability.
